name: What are red flags to watch for when hiring a general contractor?
headline: What are red flags to watch for when hiring a general contractor?

mainEntity.name: What are red flags to watch for when hiring a general contractor?
mainEntity.text: What are red flags to watch for when hiring a general contractor?

acceptedAnswer.text: When you’re interviewing general contractors, there are a few big red flags that should make you slow down or walk away. Be cautious if a contractor: - Can’t or won’t give you a valid California license number - Refuses to show proof of insurance - Only wants to be paid in cash or asks you to write checks to an individual instead of the business - Asks for a very large deposit upfront or full payment before work is done - Pushes you to skip permits to “save time or money” - Won’t give you a written contract or gives you one that’s extremely vague - Gets defensive or angry when you ask basic questions about schedule, costs, or references - Has mostly recent negative reviews or no local references in places like Pacifica, Daly City, or San Bruno Another warning sign is poor communication before you even hire them: repeatedly missing calls, showing up late to meetings, or constantly changing their story. If you see one or two minor concerns, bring them up directly and see how they respond. If you see several of these issues together, you’re usually better off finding another contractor. It’s easier to spend a little more time searching now than to be stuck in a stressful project with someone you don’t trust.
